pages.title: Agent Lifespan Engineering
pages.summary: Treating deployed agentic systems as objects whose effectiveness degrades over a deployment horizon, with memory policy — not model strength — as the dominant variable; the underlying frame of the 2026-05-29 AgingBench paper.
pages.description: Treating deployed agentic systems as objects whose effectiveness degrades over a deployment horizon, with memory policy — not model strength — as the dominant variable; the underlying frame of the 2026-05-29 AgingBench paper.

pages.title: Agentic AI Security Crisis
pages.summary: The 2026 storyline of agentic AI systems — MCP servers, agent frameworks, and integrated runtimes — surfacing a new class of supply-chain and capability-misuse vulnerabilities at scale.
pages.description: The 2026 storyline of agentic AI systems — MCP servers, agent frameworks, and integrated runtimes — surfacing a new class of supply-chain and capability-misuse vulnerabilities at scale.

pages.title: AGIBOT
pages.summary: Chinese humanoid-robotics maker that unveiled four new embodied-AI products at WAIC 2026, led by the A3 Ultra humanoid, claiming ~39% of the global humanoid-robot market.
pages.description: Chinese humanoid-robotics maker that unveiled four new embodied-AI products at WAIC 2026, led by the A3 Ultra humanoid, claiming ~39% of the global humanoid-robot market.

pages.title: The AI Capex Supercycle
pages.summary: The cross-cutting narrative of a historically large, debt- and equity-financed buildout of GPU/TPU compute capacity, and the question of whether demand justifies it.
pages.description: The cross-cutting narrative of a historically large, debt- and equity-financed buildout of GPU/TPU compute capacity, and the question of whether demand justifies it.

images.alt: Rows of datacenter server racks used to depict the AI infrastructure buildout behind the capex supercycle.
images.caption: Server-rack imagery for the physical infrastructure layer of the AI-capex supercycle.

images.alt: Rear view of dense datacenter racks and cabling, illustrating the physical compute layer behind AI-capex demand.
images.caption: Dense rack cabling as a concrete depiction of the power, networking, and compute layer behind AI infrastructure.

pages.title: Alibaba
pages.summary: Chinese tech conglomerate (Alibaba Cloud, Qwen models) accused by Anthropic of running the largest documented Claude-distillation campaign; confirmed the 2.4T-param Qwen3.8-Max is going open-weight soon, even as it continues fighting a US DoD PLA-linked designation.
pages.description: Chinese tech conglomerate (Alibaba Cloud, Qwen models) accused by Anthropic of running the largest documented Claude-distillation campaign; confirmed the 2.4T-param Qwen3.8-Max is going open-weight soon, even as it continues fighting a US DoD PLA-linked designation.

pages.title: Amazon
pages.summary: Hyperscaler, Anthropic's single largest investor, and — per WSJ/Axios reporting — the trigger of the June 2026 Fable 5 / Mythos 5 export crackdown after CEO Andy Jassy briefed Treasury that Amazon researchers had jailbroken the model.
pages.description: Hyperscaler, Anthropic's single largest investor, and — per WSJ/Axios reporting — the trigger of the June 2026 Fable 5 / Mythos 5 export crackdown after CEO Andy Jassy briefed Treasury that Amazon researchers had jailbroken the model.

pages.title: Anthropic
pages.summary: AI safety company and frontier lab behind the Claude model family; closed a $65B Series H at $965B post-money on 2026-05-28; in early talks to lease up to $10B in compute from Meta over two years and in bank credit-line talks worth "a few billions" ahead of a planned IPO (2026-07-19).
pages.description: AI safety company and frontier lab behind the Claude model family; closed a $65B Series H at $965B post-money on 2026-05-28; in early talks to lease up to $10B in compute from Meta over two years and in bank credit-line talks worth "a few billions" ahead of a planned IPO (2026-07-19).

pages.title: Apple
pages.summary: Consumer-hardware giant whose long-delayed Siri rebuild — reportedly powered by a custom Google Gemini model with a user-selectable "Extensions" layer — is the marquee AI item at WWDC 2026; sued OpenAI (2026-07-12) over alleged hardware trade-secret theft, escalating to legal letters against dozens of OpenAI employees (2026-07-18).
pages.description: Consumer-hardware giant whose long-delayed Siri rebuild — reportedly powered by a custom Google Gemini model with a user-selectable "Extensions" layer — is the marquee AI item at WWDC 2026; sued OpenAI (2026-07-12) over alleged hardware trade-secret theft, escalating to legal letters against dozens of OpenAI employees (2026-07-18).

pages.title: Artificial General Engineer
pages.summary: The thesis that an AI system can take complex physical products from concept through production — extending AI's reach from software and chatbots into hardware design and manufacturing.
pages.description: The thesis that an AI system can take complex physical products from concept through production — extending AI's reach from software and chatbots into hardware design and manufacturing.

pages.title: Broadcom
pages.summary: Custom AI ASIC vendor and capex sentiment lever; central to Google/Anthropic TPU financing, OpenAI's first custom inference chip (Jalapeño, co-designed with Broadcom), and the June 2026 Broadcom guidance gut-check that erased roughly $320B of market value.
pages.description: Custom AI ASIC vendor and capex sentiment lever; central to Google/Anthropic TPU financing, OpenAI's first custom inference chip (Jalapeño, co-designed with Broadcom), and the June 2026 Broadcom guidance gut-check that erased roughly $320B of market value.

pages.title: California AI Regulation
pages.summary: The 2026 storyline of California acting as the operative US AI regulator while the federal AI executive order remains pulled — anchored by the Transparency in Frontier AI Act and a ~30-bill package that cleared the May 29 chamber-of-origin crossover.
pages.description: The 2026 storyline of California acting as the operative US AI regulator while the federal AI executive order remains pulled — anchored by the Transparency in Frontier AI Act and a ~30-bill package that cleared the May 29 chamber-of-origin crossover.
aliases: California Transparency in Frontier AI Act

pages.title: Chai Discovery
pages.summary: AI-drug-discovery startup that raised a $400M Series C at a $3.8B valuation (2026-07-15) — nearly 3x its December valuation — for AI-designed molecules already used by Eli Lilly, Novartis, and Pfizer.
pages.description: AI-drug-discovery startup that raised a $400M Series C at a $3.8B valuation (2026-07-15) — nearly 3x its December valuation — for AI-designed molecules already used by Eli Lilly, Novartis, and Pfizer.

pages.title: China AI Regulation
pages.summary: The 2026 storyline of Beijing regulating AI on two axes — domestically (the AI companion law that forced Doubao and Qwen offline) and internationally (Xi Jinping's 2026-07-17 World AI Conference keynote pushing a Shanghai-headquartered World AI Cooperation Organization).
pages.description: The 2026 storyline of Beijing regulating AI on two axes — domestically (the AI companion law that forced Doubao and Qwen offline) and internationally (Xi Jinping's 2026-07-17 World AI Conference keynote pushing a Shanghai-headquartered World AI Cooperation Organization).
aliases: Interim Measures for the Administration of AI Anthropomorphic Interactive Services

pages.title: Claude Fable 5 / Mythos 5
pages.summary: Anthropic's 2026-06-09 frontier release — one Mythos-class model sold as two products, the safeguarded GA Fable 5 (auto-routing high-risk queries to Opus 4.8) and the restricted, unsafeguarded Mythos 5 for Glasswing/critical-infra partners; returned worldwide 2026-07-01 after a two-week export ban, metered and reclassified, and promptly topped the Remote Labor Index.
pages.description: Anthropic's 2026-06-09 frontier release — one Mythos-class model sold as two products, the safeguarded GA Fable 5 (auto-routing high-risk queries to Opus 4.8) and the restricted, unsafeguarded Mythos 5 for Glasswing/critical-infra partners; returned worldwide 2026-07-01 after a two-week export ban, metered and reclassified, and promptly topped the Remote Labor Index.

pages.title: Claude Opus 4.8
pages.summary: Anthropic's 2026-05-28 frontier flagship, succeeding Opus 4.7 at flat pricing with a +4.9pp agentic-coding lift, +137 knowledge-work Elo, and a Fast Mode that runs ~2.5× faster and 3× cheaper.
pages.description: Anthropic's 2026-05-28 frontier flagship, succeeding Opus 4.7 at flat pricing with a +4.9pp agentic-coding lift, +137 knowledge-work Elo, and a Fast Mode that runs ~2.5× faster and 3× cheaper.

pages.title: Claude Science
pages.summary: Anthropic's agentic research workbench launched 2026-06-30 — 60+ scientific tools, on-demand managed compute, a citation/calculation verification agent, and local/HPC execution — debuting with Novartis, Bristol Myers Squibb, and Genentech.
pages.description: Anthropic's agentic research workbench launched 2026-06-30 — 60+ scientific tools, on-demand managed compute, a citation/calculation verification agent, and local/HPC execution — debuting with Novartis, Bristol Myers Squibb, and Genentech.

pages.title: Claude Sonnet 5
pages.summary: Anthropic's 2026-06-30 mid-tier model — now the default on Free and Pro, priced $2/$10 per M (intro) with a 1M-token context — positioned as a cheap agentic default, whose day-2 independent benchmarks landed mixed and whose cyber capability was deliberately reduced.
pages.description: Anthropic's 2026-06-30 mid-tier model — now the default on Free and Pro, priced $2/$10 per M (intro) with a 1M-token context — positioned as a cheap agentic default, whose day-2 independent benchmarks landed mixed and whose cyber capability was deliberately reduced.

pages.title: Cognition AI
pages.summary: Maker of the Devin autonomous coding agent; raised $1B+ at a $26B post-money valuation in May 2026 against $492M ARR (~13× YoY), with 89% of Cognition's own code now written by Devin.
pages.description: Maker of the Devin autonomous coding agent; raised $1B+ at a $26B post-money valuation in May 2026 against $492M ARR (~13× YoY), with 89% of Cognition's own code now written by Devin.

pages.title: CoreWeave
pages.summary: NASDAQ-listed GPU-as-a-service neocloud with a ~$99B contracted backlog, ~$25B debt, and revenue concentrated in Microsoft, OpenAI, and Meta.
pages.description: NASDAQ-listed GPU-as-a-service neocloud with a ~$99B contracted backlog, ~$25B debt, and revenue concentrated in Microsoft, OpenAI, and Meta.

pages.title: Cursor (Anysphere)
pages.summary: AI coding tool / IDE maker Anysphere, acquired by SpaceX in a $60B all-stock deal (Q3-2026 close); the two had been jointly training a from-scratch "SpaceXAI" model.
pages.description: AI coding tool / IDE maker Anysphere, acquired by SpaceX in a $60B all-stock deal (Q3-2026 close); the two had been jointly training a from-scratch "SpaceXAI" model.

pages.title: DeepSeek
pages.summary: Hangzhou-based Chinese frontier lab; closed its first-ever external round (~$7.4B / ~50B yuan at a $50B+ valuation, the largest in Chinese AI history) on 2026-06-18 — founder-controlled, vote-less capital from Tencent + CATL, with the state AI fund the only voting investor — marking its pivot from research to commercialization toward AGI.
pages.description: Hangzhou-based Chinese frontier lab; closed its first-ever external round (~$7.4B / ~50B yuan at a $50B+ valuation, the largest in Chinese AI history) on 2026-06-18 — founder-controlled, vote-less capital from Tencent + CATL, with the state AI fund the only voting investor — marking its pivot from research to commercialization toward AGI.

pages.title: Dell Technologies
pages.summary: US enterprise-IT OEM; Q1 FY27 (reported 2026-05-29) printed $43.8B revenue (+88% YoY) with AI-Optimized Servers at $16.1B (+757% YoY), $24.4B AI orders booked, FY27 AI-server outlook raised to $60B; stock +32% on the print, the best single day in company history.
pages.description: US enterprise-IT OEM; Q1 FY27 (reported 2026-05-29) printed $43.8B revenue (+88% YoY) with AI-Optimized Servers at $16.1B (+757% YoY), $24.4B AI orders booked, FY27 AI-server outlook raised to $60B; stock +32% on the print, the best single day in company history.

pages.title: Dynamic Workflows
pages.summary: Anthropic's 2026-05-28 Claude Code feature in which Claude writes a JavaScript orchestrator that fans a single session out to up to 1,000 parallel subagents per run, gated to Enterprise / Team / Max tiers and aimed at codebase-scale migrations.
pages.description: Anthropic's 2026-05-28 Claude Code feature in which Claude writes a JavaScript orchestrator that fans a single session out to up to 1,000 parallel subagents per run, gated to Enterprise / Team / Max tiers and aimed at codebase-scale migrations.

pages.title: Etched
pages.summary: AI-chip startup that exited stealth on 2026-06-30 with $800M raised, $1B+ in signed contracts, and a $5B valuation — betting on Sohu, a transformer-only inference ASIC whose first racks ship summer 2026 (performance claims still vendor-sourced).
pages.description: AI-chip startup that exited stealth on 2026-06-30 with $800M raised, $1B+ in signed contracts, and a $5B valuation — betting on Sohu, a transformer-only inference ASIC whose first racks ship summer 2026 (performance claims still vendor-sourced).

pages.title: Federal AI Policy
pages.summary: The 2026 storyline of US federal AI governance — Trump's June 2 executive order mandating 30-day government pre-release access to frontier models for cybersecurity review, hardening by late June 2026 into a de facto licensing regime with no rules, now visibly gating both US frontier flagships and structurally excluding US allies.
pages.description: The 2026 storyline of US federal AI governance — Trump's June 2 executive order mandating 30-day government pre-release access to frontier models for cybersecurity review, hardening by late June 2026 into a de facto licensing regime with no rules, now visibly gating both US frontier flagships and structurally excluding US allies.
aliases: Promoting Advanced AI Innovation and Security

pages.title: Figure AI
pages.summary: US humanoid-robotics company whose Helix-02 in-house VLA ran a 200-hour fully autonomous package-sort shift (249,560 packages, zero hardware failures, zero teleop) on its Figure 03 fleet at Sunnyvale on 2026-05-30, ~10× the previous public envelope on MTBI.
pages.description: US humanoid-robotics company whose Helix-02 in-house VLA ran a 200-hour fully autonomous package-sort shift (249,560 packages, zero hardware failures, zero teleop) on its Figure 03 fleet at Sunnyvale on 2026-05-30, ~10× the previous public envelope on MTBI.

pages.title: Fireworks AI
pages.summary: AI inference-infrastructure company that raised a $1.505B Series D at a $17.5B valuation (2026-07-18), citing 40T+ tokens/day served — one of the largest inference-infra rounds of the cycle.
pages.description: AI inference-infrastructure company that raised a $1.505B Series D at a $17.5B valuation (2026-07-18), citing 40T+ tokens/day served — one of the largest inference-infra rounds of the cycle.

pages.title: Gemini 3.5 Flash
pages.summary: Google's fast, low-cost frontier model shipped GA at Google I/O 2026, positioned for agents rather than chatbots; gained a real-time speech-to-speech Live Translate variant (70+ languages) on 2026-06-09.
pages.description: Google's fast, low-cost frontier model shipped GA at Google I/O 2026, positioned for agents rather than chatbots; gained a real-time speech-to-speech Live Translate variant (70+ languages) on 2026-06-09.

pages.title: Gemini 3.5 Pro
pages.summary: Google DeepMind's Pro-tier follow-on to Gemini 3.5 Flash, confirmed at I/O 2026 but reported months behind schedule after coding capability fell short of internal targets — wiping out ~$200B of Alphabet's market cap on the news.
pages.description: Google DeepMind's Pro-tier follow-on to Gemini 3.5 Flash, confirmed at I/O 2026 but reported months behind schedule after coding capability fell short of internal targets — wiping out ~$200B of Alphabet's market cap on the news.

pages.title: Gemini 3.6 Flash, 3.5 Flash-Lite & Flash Cyber
pages.summary: Google's 2026-07-21 triple Flash-tier release — Gemini 3.6 Flash, 3.5 Flash-Lite, and a security-focused "Cyber" variant — shipped while Gemini 3.5 Pro remains delayed and Gemini 4 is already in training.
pages.description: Google's 2026-07-21 triple Flash-tier release — Gemini 3.6 Flash, 3.5 Flash-Lite, and a security-focused "Cyber" variant — shipped while Gemini 3.5 Pro remains delayed and Gemini 4 is already in training.

pages.title: Gemini Spark
pages.summary: Google's persistent consumer agent for Gemini, hit GA on 2026-05-29 for US AI Ultra subscribers at $99.99/mo — the first GA paid frontier-lab persistent consumer agent, running on dedicated Google Cloud VMs so state persists with the phone off.
pages.description: Google's persistent consumer agent for Gemini, hit GA on 2026-05-29 for US AI Ultra subscribers at $99.99/mo — the first GA paid frontier-lab persistent consumer agent, running on dedicated Google Cloud VMs so state persists with the phone off.

pages.title: Gemma 4
pages.summary: Google DeepMind's open-weights (Apache 2.0) multimodal model family; the 2026-06-04 cycle surfaced an encoder-free 12B variant that runs locally on 16 GB RAM as Gemma crossed 150M+ cumulative downloads.
pages.description: Google DeepMind's open-weights (Apache 2.0) multimodal model family; the 2026-06-04 cycle surfaced an encoder-free 12B variant that runs locally on 16 GB RAM as Gemma crossed 150M+ cumulative downloads.

pages.title: Google
pages.summary: Hyperscaler and frontier-model builder behind Gemini; ordered by the EU under the DMA to open Android and Search to rivals (2026-07-17), the same day it rebranded NotebookLM into the Gemini product family.
pages.description: Hyperscaler and frontier-model builder behind Gemini; ordered by the EU under the DMA to open Android and Search to rivals (2026-07-17), the same day it rebranded NotebookLM into the Gemini product family.

pages.title: OpenAI GPT-5.6
pages.summary: OpenAI frontier family — Sol (flagship) / Terra (balanced) / Luna (fast/cheap); the full family is now GA, powering the new "ChatGPT Work" product, but unprotected "Full Access Mode" reportedly deleted users' home directories in several cases.
pages.description: OpenAI frontier family — Sol (flagship) / Terra (balanced) / Luna (fast/cheap); the full family is now GA, powering the new "ChatGPT Work" product, but unprotected "Full Access Mode" reportedly deleted users' home directories in several cases.

pages.title: Jelani Nelson
pages.summary: Theoretical computer scientist and chair of UC Berkeley's EECS department who joined Anthropic as a Member of Technical Staff (on leave from Berkeley) on 2026-07-02.
pages.description: Theoretical computer scientist and chair of UC Berkeley's EECS department who joined Anthropic as a Member of Technical Staff (on leave from Berkeley) on 2026-07-02.

pages.title: John Jumper
pages.summary: AlphaFold co-creator and joint 2024 Nobel laureate in Chemistry who left Google DeepMind for Anthropic on 2026-06-20 — the most prestigious science hire of the frontier-talent war.
pages.description: AlphaFold co-creator and joint 2024 Nobel laureate in Chemistry who left Google DeepMind for Anthropic on 2026-06-20 — the most prestigious science hire of the frontier-talent war.

pages.title: LifeSciBench
pages.summary: OpenAI's 750-task, expert-authored life-science research benchmark (released 2026-06-17) on which the best model clears only 36.1% — a concrete measure of the gap between frontier models and autonomous scientific research.
pages.description: OpenAI's 750-task, expert-authored life-science research benchmark (released 2026-06-17) on which the best model clears only 36.1% — a concrete measure of the gap between frontier models and autonomous scientific research.

pages.title: Liquid AI
pages.summary: MIT-spinout foundation model lab focused on on-device hybrid (non-pure-transformer) architectures; shipped LFM2.5-8B-A1B (8B/1B-active MoE, 128K context, 38T pre-training tokens) with day-one llama.cpp / MLX / vLLM / SGLang support on 2026-05-29.
pages.description: MIT-spinout foundation model lab focused on on-device hybrid (non-pure-transformer) architectures; shipped LFM2.5-8B-A1B (8B/1B-active MoE, 128K context, 38T pre-training tokens) with day-one llama.cpp / MLX / vLLM / SGLang support on 2026-05-29.

pages.title: Meituan LongCat-2.0
pages.summary: Meituan's 2026-06-30 open-weighted 1.6T-param MoE coding model, ~1M context, that Meituan says was pretrained on a ~50,000-chip all-domestic (no-Nvidia) cluster — and revealed as the anonymous "Owl Alpha" that had topped OpenRouter coding usage for ~two months.
pages.description: Meituan's 2026-06-30 open-weighted 1.6T-param MoE coding model, ~1M context, that Meituan says was pretrained on a ~50,000-chip all-domestic (no-Nvidia) cluster — and revealed as the anonymous "Owl Alpha" that had topped OpenRouter coding usage for ~two months.

pages.title: Meta
pages.summary: Social-platform hyperscaler and frontier-model builder (Llama); began alerting parents when teens discuss suicide/self-harm with Meta AI (2026-07-17, live in the US/UK/Australia/Canada), its most direct AI-safety product response yet.
pages.description: Social-platform hyperscaler and frontier-model builder (Llama); began alerting parents when teens discuss suicide/self-harm with Meta AI (2026-07-17, live in the US/UK/Australia/Canada), its most direct AI-safety product response yet.

pages.title: Micron Technology
pages.summary: US memory-chip maker (HBM/DRAM/SSD) that on 2026-06-23 became Anthropic's primary memory supplier under a multi-year supply + co-design pact and participated in Anthropic's Series H — the first named investor in that round.
pages.description: US memory-chip maker (HBM/DRAM/SSD) that on 2026-06-23 became Anthropic's primary memory supplier under a multi-year supply + co-design pact and participated in Anthropic's Series H — the first named investor in that round.

pages.title: Microsoft
pages.summary: Hyperscaler and frontier-model builder; at Build 2026 shipped a full first-party MAI model stack and made Project Polaris the default GitHub Copilot engine; CEO Satya Nadella publicly called Anthropic's Fable "editorially controlled" (2026-07-18) despite Microsoft's $5B stake in Anthropic.
pages.description: Hyperscaler and frontier-model builder; at Build 2026 shipped a full first-party MAI model stack and made Project Polaris the default GitHub Copilot engine; CEO Satya Nadella publicly called Anthropic's Fable "editorially controlled" (2026-07-18) despite Microsoft's $5B stake in Anthropic.

pages.title: Midjourney
pages.summary: Investor-free, community-funded AI image-generation lab (~$100M revenue in its first 9 months) now diversifying into hardware (a full-body ultrasound scanner under Midjourney Medical) while seeking discovery into Disney/Universal/Warner Bros.' own internal AI-training practices in its copyright suit.
pages.description: Investor-free, community-funded AI image-generation lab (~$100M revenue in its first 9 months) now diversifying into hardware (a full-body ultrasound scanner under Midjourney Medical) while seeking discovery into Disney/Universal/Warner Bros.' own internal AI-training practices in its copyright suit.

pages.title: MiniMax M3
pages.summary: MiniMax's open-weights model with 1M context and 59% SWE-Bench Pro — the strongest open-weights agentic-coding model of the cycle.
pages.description: MiniMax's open-weights model with 1M context and 59% SWE-Bench Pro — the strongest open-weights agentic-coding model of the cycle.

pages.title: Mistral Leanstral 1.5
pages.summary: Mistral's open-source (Apache 2.0) formal-verification model for Lean 4 — saturates miniF2F, sets SOTA on FATE-H/FATE-X, and found 5 previously-unknown bugs across 57 scanned open-source repos; the day's top Hacker News AI story.
pages.description: Mistral's open-source (Apache 2.0) formal-verification model for Lean 4 — saturates miniF2F, sets SOTA on FATE-H/FATE-X, and found 5 previously-unknown bugs across 57 scanned open-source repos; the day's top Hacker News AI story.

pages.title: Mistral Robostral Navigate
pages.summary: Mistral AI's first embodied-navigation model — an 8B-parameter model that guides robots to autonomously perform natural-language-specified tasks, announced 2026-07-08.
pages.description: Mistral AI's first embodied-navigation model — an 8B-parameter model that guides robots to autonomously perform natural-language-specified tasks, announced 2026-07-08.

pages.title: Moonshot Kimi K2.7 Code
pages.summary: Moonshot AI's open coding model that undercuts GPT-5.5 and Claude by up to roughly 12x on price per token while staying competitive; one of the Chinese open-weight releases filling the Fable 5 vacuum.
pages.description: Moonshot AI's open coding model that undercuts GPT-5.5 and Claude by up to roughly 12x on price per token while staying competitive; one of the Chinese open-weight releases filling the Fable 5 vacuum.

pages.title: Moonshot AI Kimi K3
pages.summary: Moonshot AI's 2.8T-parameter flagship ("Open Frontier Intelligence"); pending a $30B+ Hong Kong IPO on breakout demand, even as GPU strain forced a new-subscriber pause and a contested ECI methodology now reads the US-China gap as widening, not narrowing.
pages.description: Moonshot AI's 2.8T-parameter flagship ("Open Frontier Intelligence"); pending a $30B+ Hong Kong IPO on breakout demand, even as GPU strain forced a new-subscriber pause and a contested ECI methodology now reads the US-China gap as widening, not narrowing.

pages.title: Nano Banana 2 Lite
pages.summary: Google's fastest/cheapest image model (gemini-3.1-flash-lite-image), live 2026-06-30 at ~4s and ~$0.034/image — shipped alongside Gemini Omni Flash, which brings text-prompt video generation/editing to the Gemini API for the first time.
pages.description: Google's fastest/cheapest image model (gemini-3.1-flash-lite-image), live 2026-06-30 at ~4s and ~$0.034/image — shipped alongside Gemini Omni Flash, which brings text-prompt video generation/editing to the Gemini API for the first time.

pages.title: Nebius Group
pages.summary: Amsterdam-based AI cloud ("neocloud") provider spun out of Yandex, scaling GPU capacity for AI training and inference; signed a $1B+ Nvidia GB300 compute deal with Reflection AI through 2029 (2026-07-15), though its stock fell ~5% on the news.
pages.description: Amsterdam-based AI cloud ("neocloud") provider spun out of Yandex, scaling GPU capacity for AI training and inference; signed a $1B+ Nvidia GB300 compute deal with Reflection AI through 2029 (2026-07-15), though its stock fell ~5% on the news.

pages.title: Neocloud
pages.summary: Business model where a specialized provider buys GPUs on debt and rents the capacity back under multi-year take-or-pay contracts, distinct from general-purpose hyperscalers.
pages.description: Business model where a specialized provider buys GPUs on debt and rents the capacity back under multi-year take-or-pay contracts, distinct from general-purpose hyperscalers.

pages.title: Noam Shazeer
pages.summary: Transformer co-inventor ("Attention Is All You Need"), Character.AI founder, and former Google Gemini co-lead who left Google for OpenAI as Lead for Architecture Research on 2026-06-18.
pages.description: Transformer co-inventor ("Attention Is All You Need"), Character.AI founder, and former Google Gemini co-lead who left Google for OpenAI as Lead for Architecture Research on 2026-06-18.

pages.title: NVIDIA
pages.summary: The dominant AI accelerator supplier; its GPUs underwrite the AI-capex buildout, and on 2026-06-05 it released the open-weights Nemotron-3-Ultra-550B that dominated developer mindshare alongside Gemma 4.
pages.description: The dominant AI accelerator supplier; its GPUs underwrite the AI-capex buildout, and on 2026-06-05 it released the open-weights Nemotron-3-Ultra-550B that dominated developer mindshare alongside Gemma 4.

images.alt: Close-up of an NVIDIA H100 accelerator used to depict NVIDIA's role in AI training and inference.
images.caption: NVIDIA H100 hardware, the accelerator class at the center of the AI-capex buildout.

images.alt: NVIDIA DGX Station deskside AI supercomputer shown as an example of NVIDIA's local and datacenter AI systems.
images.caption: DGX Station-class hardware, useful context for the page's deskside AI-supercomputer discussion.

pages.title: The Open-Weights Wave
pages.summary: The 2026 storyline of open-weight models closing on frontier capability while a decentralization backlash — torrent networks, local hosting, "APIs are rented, weights are forever" — gains force, surging directly on the Fable 5 government shutdown and hardening through mid-2026 as a broker/router shift routes production traffic to Chinese open models.
pages.description: The 2026 storyline of open-weight models closing on frontier capability while a decentralization backlash — torrent networks, local hosting, "APIs are rented, weights are forever" — gains force, surging directly on the Fable 5 government shutdown and hardening through mid-2026 as a broker/router shift routes production traffic to Chinese open models.

pages.title: OpenAI
pages.summary: AI lab behind the GPT family; March 2026 private valuation of $852B (now exceeded by Anthropic's $965B); sued by Apple over trade-secret theft (2026-07-12), and now the target of Apple legal letters to dozens of its employees (2026-07-18), amid a GPT-5.6 "Full Access Mode" file-deletion safety incident.
pages.description: AI lab behind the GPT family; March 2026 private valuation of $852B (now exceeded by Anthropic's $965B); sued by Apple over trade-secret theft (2026-07-12), and now the target of Apple legal letters to dozens of its employees (2026-07-18), amid a GPT-5.6 "Full Access Mode" file-deletion safety incident.

pages.title: OpenRouter
pages.summary: LLM-routing API serving 400+ models to 8M+ developers; closed a $113M Series B at $1.3B post-money on 2026-05-30 and later surfaced Fusion API, a model-fusion endpoint blending outputs across providers.
pages.description: LLM-routing API serving 400+ models to 8M+ developers; closed a $113M Series B at $1.3B post-money on 2026-05-30 and later surfaced Fusion API, a model-fusion endpoint blending outputs across providers.

pages.title: Prometheus
pages.summary: Jeff Bezos's industrial-AI startup building an "artificial general engineer"; disclosed a $12B Series B at a ~$41B valuation on 2026-06-11 — industrial AI's first megaround.
pages.description: Jeff Bezos's industrial-AI startup building an "artificial general engineer"; disclosed a $12B Series B at a ~$41B valuation on 2026-06-11 — industrial AI's first megaround.

pages.title: Remote Labor Index (RLI)
pages.summary: Scale AI × Center for AI Safety benchmark measuring how much real, paid remote freelance work AI agents can automate end-to-end; the leading model tops out near 16% while still failing the large majority of tasks.
pages.description: Scale AI × Center for AI Safety benchmark measuring how much real, paid remote freelance work AI agents can automate end-to-end; the leading model tops out near 16% while still failing the large majority of tasks.

pages.title: Sakana AI
pages.summary: Tokyo-based AI lab whose multi-model-orchestration thesis ships as Marlin (an autonomous "Ultra Deep Research" agent) and Sakana Fugu (an LLM-as-router that calls a swappable pool of frontier models) — a non-US entrant pitching orchestration as a workaround to the Fable 5 export freeze.
pages.description: Tokyo-based AI lab whose multi-model-orchestration thesis ships as Marlin (an autonomous "Ultra Deep Research" agent) and Sakana Fugu (an LLM-as-router that calls a swappable pool of frontier models) — a non-US entrant pitching orchestration as a workaround to the Fable 5 export freeze.

pages.title: Salesforce
pages.summary: Enterprise CRM giant betting its AI future on agentic software (Agentforce); acquired AI customer-service platform Fin (formerly Intercom) for $3.6B on 2026-06-15 — the day's largest confirmed AI M&A.
pages.description: Enterprise CRM giant betting its AI future on agentic software (Agentforce); acquired AI customer-service platform Fin (formerly Intercom) for $3.6B on 2026-06-15 — the day's largest confirmed AI M&A.

pages.title: SK Hynix
pages.summary: South Korean HBM memory supplier behind Nvidia's AI accelerators; raised $26.5B via a Nasdaq listing on 2026-07-11, the largest US share sale ever by a foreign company.
pages.description: South Korean HBM memory supplier behind Nvidia's AI accelerators; raised $26.5B via a Nasdaq listing on 2026-07-11, the largest US share sale ever by a foreign company.

pages.title: SK Telecom
pages.summary: South Korea's largest wireless carrier and a ~$100M Anthropic investor; named by WIRED on 2026-06-21 as the Project Glasswing partner whose Mythos 5 access — flagged over suspected China ties — helped trigger the US Fable 5 / Mythos 5 export suspension.
pages.description: South Korea's largest wireless carrier and a ~$100M Anthropic investor; named by WIRED on 2026-06-21 as the Project Glasswing partner whose Mythos 5 access — flagged over suspected China ties — helped trigger the US Fable 5 / Mythos 5 export suspension.

pages.title: Soofi S 30B-A3B
pages.summary: Deutsche Telekom-backed sovereign open German/English MoE foundation model trained on ~27T tokens, claimed strongest fully-open model on combined English+German benchmarks.
pages.description: Deutsche Telekom-backed sovereign open German/English MoE foundation model trained on ~27T tokens, claimed strongest fully-open model on combined English+German benchmarks.

pages.title: SpaceX
pages.summary: Aerospace company whose record IPO (Nasdaq SPCX, pricing 2026-06-11 at $135/share, ~$1.75T valuation, ~$74.4B net raise) anchors the 2026 AI-financing issuance wave on the public-markets side.
pages.description: Aerospace company whose record IPO (Nasdaq SPCX, pricing 2026-06-11 at $135/share, ~$1.75T valuation, ~$74.4B net raise) anchors the 2026 AI-financing issuance wave on the public-markets side.

images.alt: SpaceX Falcon 9 launch used to depict the aerospace company behind the SPCX public-markets story.
images.caption: Falcon 9 launch imagery grounds the SpaceX page in the company's core aerospace business.

images.alt: SpaceX Starship ignition during an integrated flight test, depicting the heavy-launch program behind SpaceX's valuation story.
images.caption: Starship launch-system imagery helps contextualize the scale behind the SpaceX valuation and AI-infrastructure financing narrative.

pages.title: Tencent Hunyuan Hy3
pages.summary: Tencent Hunyuan's 295B-parameter open-weight MoE model under Apache 2.0, reported new open SOTA on the MCP-Atlas benchmark, released 2026-07-06.
pages.description: Tencent Hunyuan's 295B-parameter open-weight MoE model under Apache 2.0, reported new open SOTA on the MCP-Atlas benchmark, released 2026-07-06.

pages.title: Thinking Machines Lab
pages.summary: Mira Murati's AI lab; shipped Inkling on 2026-07-15, its first public model release — a 975B-parameter (41B active) open-weights multimodal MoE positioned against both Chinese open labs and closed US frontier players.
pages.description: Mira Murati's AI lab; shipped Inkling on 2026-07-15, its first public model release — a 975B-parameter (41B active) open-weights multimodal MoE positioned against both Chinese open labs and closed US frontier players.

pages.title: xAI
pages.summary: Elon Musk's frontier lab behind Grok; shipped Grok Build 0.2.105 with Grok 4.5 as the new default model (2026-07-19), while still suing users over Grok-generated CSAM (2026-07-17) rather than addressing the underlying capability.
pages.description: Elon Musk's frontier lab behind Grok; shipped Grok Build 0.2.105 with Grok 4.5 as the new default model (2026-07-19), while still suing users over Grok-generated CSAM (2026-07-17) rather than addressing the underlying capability.

pages.title: Xiaomi MiMo-v2.5-Pro-UltraSpeed
pages.summary: Xiaomi's MiMo-v2.5-Pro-UltraSpeed claim of 1,000+ tokens/sec on a 1T-parameter MoE using a standard 8-GPU server; a high-signal but still partially verified inference-efficiency story.
pages.description: Xiaomi's MiMo-v2.5-Pro-UltraSpeed claim of 1,000+ tokens/sec on a 1T-parameter MoE using a standard 8-GPU server; a high-signal but still partially verified inference-efficiency story.

pages.title: Zhipu GLM-5.2
pages.summary: Zhipu AI / Z.ai flagship model shipped under an MIT license with 1M context and two reasoning-effort levels; the marquee long-context Chinese open-weight release in the Fable 5 shutdown window — and, late June, the subject of a debunked "matches Mythos" cybersecurity headline.
